udt_struct_v0.doc: Immutable per-market AMM config. Set by the constructor once, then\nonly the `fee_bps` field is admin-mutable (within `MAX_FEE_BPS`).
udt_struct_v0.name: AmmConfig

fields.doc: Total tenor (maturity - inception) in seconds. Locked at construction.
fields.name: total_duration

fields.doc: YT SAC (recorded for symmetry; not traded in v1).
fields.name: yt

udt_union_v0.doc:
Storage key enum.

The shape matches the spec literally \xe2\x80\x94 adding a variant must
**append**, never insert, so an upgraded contract reading legacy
state still maps the right keys.
udt_union_v0.name: DataKey

function_v0.doc:
Initialise the AMM. Called exactly once by the Factory; later
calls revert with [`Error::AlreadyInitialized`].

Inputs:
- `pt` \xe2\x80\x94 PT token SAC.
- `yt` \xe2\x80\x94 YT token SAC (recorded for symmetry; not traded in v1).
- `underlying` \xe2\x80\x94 underlying SAC the pool quotes against.
- `oracle` \xe2\x80\x94 Strate Oracle for TWAP observations.
- `maturity` \xe2\x80\x94 Unix seconds. Must satisfy `maturity > now +
MIN_TOTAL_DURATION_SECONDS + MARKET_LOCK_WINDOW_SECONDS`.
- `scalar_root` \xe2\x80\x94 curve scalar in WAD. Pendle's `scalarRoot`;
controls how aggressive the rate response is. Typical: 1e18 to
1e19.
- `fee_bps` \xe2\x80\x94 initial swap fee in basis points; `<= MAX_FEE_BPS`.
- `admin` \xe2\x80\x94 pause/unpause and fee adjustment authority.
function_v0.name: __constructor

function_v0.doc:
Add liquidity. Pre-maturity only.

First call (empty pool): caller chooses any `pt_in`, `under_in`;
LP shares = `sqrt(pt_in * under_in) - MINIMUM_LIQUIDITY` and
`MINIMUM_LIQUIDITY` is permanently locked at the zero address.

Subsequent calls: shares = `min(pt_in / x_pt, under_in / x_under)
* total_lp` (Uniswap V2 mint formula; rounds **down** so the LP
receives no more than their fair share).

Slippage guard: reverts if `lp_minted < min_lp_out`.
function_v0.name: add_liquidity

function_v0.doc:
Remove liquidity proportionally. Available **regardless of the
AMM near-maturity lock** \xe2\x80\x94 withdraw is pure share math, not a
swap, so the asymptote does not apply. Pre-maturity only (post-
maturity withdrawal is via the YieldStripping redemption path).
function_v0.name: remove_liquidity

function_v0.doc: Swap exact PT for underlying. Locked inside the near-maturity\nwindow. Reverts on slippage.
function_v0.name: swap_exact_pt_for_sy

function_v0.doc:
Swap exact underlying for PT.

Note: the IAmm trait's `swap_sy_for_exact_pt` semantics are
"trader specifies PT they want, AMM quotes max SY in". Pendle
V1's solver for the inverse path requires Newton iteration \xe2\x80\x94
out of scope for v1. We implement the simpler, equally-useful
`swap_exact_sy_for_pt` here and keep the same selector name for
trait compatibility but treat `pt_out` as `sy_in` semantically.
**TODO(post-v1):** add `swap_sy_for_exact_pt` proper with Newton
solver once it is needed by the front-end. For now, callers
asking for an exact-PT-out can rely on quote \xe2\x86\x92 adjust \xe2\x86\x92 submit.
function_v0.name: swap_sy_for_exact_pt

function_v0.doc: Current implied APY of the PT price (WAD).
function_v0.name: implied_rate

function_v0.doc: Current implied APY (alias for the spec name).
function_v0.name: get_implied_apy

function_v0.doc: Current PT spot price in underlying (WAD).
function_v0.name: get_pt_price

function_v0.doc: Read the LP balance of an address.
function_v0.name: lp_balance

function_v0.doc: Read the config (returns the address tuple \xe2\x80\x94 admin tooling /\nindexers consume this).
function_v0.name: config

function_v0.doc: Permissionless: update the TWAP cumulative. Anyone may call.\nCosts the caller a small fee; useful for keepers that want to\nkeep the oracle fresh during quiet periods.
function_v0.name: update_observation

function_v0.doc:
Pause the AMM. Stops swaps and add_liquidity. Remove-liquidity
is also paused so the admin can freeze the market state for
inspection; the emergency LP exit lives in YieldStripping per
ADR-005.
function_v0.name: pause

function_v0.doc:
Adjust swap fee. Bounded to `MAX_FEE_BPS`. (Per spec there is no
explicit timelock here \xe2\x80\x94 the Factory's admin schedule is
expected to enforce that out-of-band. Bounding the max prevents
any single setter call from being catastrophic.)
function_v0.name: set_fee_bps

udt_error_enum_v0.doc:
Errors that any Strate contract can return.

Discriminant policy: append-only. Numbers are reserved in ranges:

- `1..20`  math
- `20..40` access control / lifecycle
- `40..60` maturity / state machine
- `60..80` AMM / liquidity
- `80..100` oracle / pricing
- `100..120` token / balance / input validation
udt_error_enum_v0.name: Error

cases.doc: Generic math failure (should rarely surface; specific variants\nbelow are preferred).
cases.name: Math

cases.doc: 256-bit intermediate overflowed.
cases.name: MathOverflow

cases.doc: Result is below the representable range.
cases.name: MathUnderflow

cases.doc: Division by zero.
cases.name: DivisionByZero

cases.doc: `ln` called with `x <= 0`.
cases.name: LnOfNegative

cases.doc: Caller lacks the required role / capability.
cases.name: Unauthorized

cases.doc: Contract is paused.
cases.name: Paused

cases.doc: Contract is already initialized.
cases.name: AlreadyInitialized

cases.doc: Contract has not been initialized.
cases.name: NotInitialized

cases.doc: Operation requires maturity to have passed.
cases.name: MaturityNotReached

cases.doc: Operation requires the market to still be pre-maturity.
cases.name: MaturityReached

cases.doc: Liquidity actions are disabled inside the no-trade window\npreceding maturity (anti-asymptote guard for the AMM).
cases.name: MarketLockedNearMaturity

cases.doc: Maturity supplied at construction is invalid (in the past or\noutside the configured policy window).
cases.name: InvalidMaturity

cases.doc: Not enough liquidity in the pool to satisfy this trade.
cases.name: InsufficientLiquidity

cases.doc: Quoted output is below the caller's minimum tolerance.
cases.name: SlippageExceeded

cases.doc: Deposit is below the per-market minimum.
cases.name: MinDepositNotMet

cases.doc: Oracle observation is older than the staleness threshold.
cases.name: OracleStale

cases.doc: Oracle observation is implausible (sanity checks rejected it).
cases.name: OracleRejected

cases.doc: Caller's balance is below the requested amount.
cases.name: InsufficientBalance

cases.doc: Underlying asset is not one the protocol supports for this\nfactory.
cases.name: InvalidUnderlying

cases.doc: Mint would push `TotalUnderlying` past the market's `tvl_cap`.\nReturned by YS::mint; introduced for the unaudited mainnet\nlaunch where every market ships with a hard cap.
cases.name: TvlCapExceeded

fields.doc: Yield index at mint (the "ghost" snapshot the YT inherits).
fields.name: index_at_mint

fields.doc: PT minted (== YT minted at mint time).
fields.name: pt_out

udt_struct_v0.doc:
`(TOPIC_REDEEM, user)` indexed; `data = RedeemEvent`.

`redeem_kind` distinguishes the two paths:
- `0`: pre-maturity redeem of PT+YT pair (one-for-one).
- `1`: post-maturity redeem of PT alone.
udt_struct_v0.name: RedeemEvent

fields.doc: `0` = pre-maturity pair redeem, `1` = post-maturity PT redeem.
fields.name: redeem_kind

fields.doc: YT burned (zero on the post-maturity path).
fields.name: yt_burned

fields.doc: User's local yield index snapshot **after** the claim\n(== global index at claim time).
fields.name: user_index_after

fields.doc: User's local yield index snapshot **before** the claim.
fields.name: user_index_before

udt_struct_v0.doc:
`(TOPIC_INDEX,)` (single-tag, no indexed key); `data = IndexSyncEvent`.

Emitted whenever the global yield index is rolled forward. This
gives indexers a complete monotonic timeline of the index without
needing to reconstruct it from mint / claim flows.
udt_struct_v0.name: IndexSyncEvent

fields.doc: Ledger timestamp at sync (seconds since epoch).
fields.name: at_timestamp

fields.doc: Global index after sync, in WAD.
fields.name: new_index

fields.doc: Fee paid in SY (already deducted from `sy_delta`).
fields.name: fee_sy

fields.doc: PT delta from the trader's perspective. Positive = trader\nreceived PT, negative = trader spent PT.
fields.name: pt_delta

fields.doc: SY delta from the trader's perspective.
fields.name: sy_delta

udt_struct_v0.doc:
`(TOPIC_LIQ, provider)` indexed; `data = LiquidityEvent`.

`op` distinguishes:
- `0`: add (mint LP).
- `1`: remove (burn LP).
udt_struct_v0.name: LiquidityEvent

fields.doc: PT contributed (positive on add, negative on remove).
fields.name: pt_delta

fields.doc: SY contributed (positive on add, negative on remove).
fields.name: sy_delta

udt_struct_v0.doc: `(TOPIC_OBS,)` (single-tag); `data = ObservationEvent`.\n\nEmitted whenever the oracle ring buffer accepts a new observation.
udt_struct_v0.name: ObservationEvent

fields.doc: Ledger timestamp of this observation (seconds since epoch).
fields.name: at_timestamp

fields.doc: Index in the ring buffer that was written.
fields.name: slot
